response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack/web developer with broad experience, but the role requires native-level Japanese, 3+ years of C on UNIX, Linux kernel modification experience, and embedded networking/security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, switch chips). The resume shows none of these core requirements — Japanese is not listed, C/C++ work was brief (~6 months at NI a decade ago), and there is no embedded networking or LAN-switch security background.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) shipping production software across multiple stacks
Some Linux and low-level exposure via DLang async I/O and a C/C++ stint at National Instruments
Strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, workshops)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — hard requirement is native-level
No embedded/firmware, LAN-switch, or network security (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP) experience
No Linux kernel investigation/modification experience; C work is minimal and dated

key_factors.factor: Minimal recent C and no Linux kernel work
